title: Next-level animations and unique design enrichment — no repeat patterns

requirements.text: Apply a new layer of unique, non-repeating micro-interaction animations to all customer-facing product cards (ProductCard.tsx) and the cart badge: add a magnetic hover tilt effect on product cards (subtle 3D perspective rotation following cursor), a liquid fill progress animation on the 'Add to Cart' button that sweeps green from left to right on click, and a numerical increment burst on the cart badge each time a new item is added. None of these animations must duplicate any existing hover, scale, or slide-up patterns already applied.

quotes: Make it more animative and designfull and don't repeat:design again

acceptanceCriteria: Product cards exhibit a subtle 3D tilt/perspective on mouse hover that is visually distinct from any existing scale or shadow transition
The 'Add to Cart' button shows a left-to-right liquid fill sweep animation on click, not a ripple or pulse
The cart badge shows a number-increment burst animation (e.g., +1 float-up) unique from the existing bounce/pulse
No animation CSS class or keyframe is shared with previously implemented animations
All new animations respect prefers-reduced-motion

requirements.text: Introduce a page-transition overlay system: when navigating between any two pages, display a brief (200–300 ms) branded curtain wipe animation (a vertical green panel that sweeps in from the left edge and immediately sweeps out to the right) before the new page renders. This must be applied globally via the Layout component and must not conflict with or duplicate existing fade-in/slide-up entrance animations already on individual pages.

quotes: Make it more animative and designfull and don't repeat:design again

acceptanceCriteria: A branded green curtain wipe plays on every route change
The wipe completes within 300 ms so it does not feel sluggish
Existing per-page entrance animations are unaffected and still play after the curtain exits
The overlay is implemented in Layout.tsx or a dedicated PageTransition component, not inside individual pages
Animation is skipped when prefers-reduced-motion is set

requirements.text: Add a dynamic scrolling progress indicator: a thin (3–4 px) emerald-to-teal gradient line at the very top of the viewport (above the Header accent bar) that fills from 0% to 100% width as the user scrolls down any page and resets on navigation. This is distinct from any existing top-border, accent bar, or underline animation already on the Header.

quotes: Make it more animative and designfull and don't repeat:design again

acceptanceCriteria: A thin gradient progress bar is visible at the absolute top of the screen on all pages
Its width accurately reflects scroll percentage (0% at top, 100% at bottom of page)
The gradient uses emerald-to-teal colors distinct from the existing Header accent bar gradient
It resets to 0% on every route change
Reduced-motion users see a static full-width bar instead of animation

requirements.text: Enhance the AnnouncementTicker (AnnouncementTicker.tsx) with a visually richer design: replace the plain scrolling strip with a frosted-glass pill-shaped ticker that has a soft inner glow, individual announcement items separated by animated dot-pulse dividers, and a subtle shimmer sweep across the background every 4 seconds. This must not reuse any animation from the existing marquee scroll or any page-body entrance animation.

quotes: Make it more animative and designfull and don't repeat:design again

acceptanceCriteria: AnnouncementTicker has a frosted-glass/pill visual style distinct from its current plain strip
Announcement items are separated by animated dot-pulse icons
A shimmer sweep plays across the ticker background on a 4-second interval
The shimmer keyframe is unique and not shared with any skeleton loader or card shimmer
prefers-reduced-motion disables the shimmer and dot-pulse while keeping the scroll

requirements.text: Add a floating ambient background layer to the HomePage hero section: render 6–8 large, very low-opacity (3–6%) morphing blob shapes in deep green and teal that slowly deform and drift using an SVG feTurbulence or CSS border-radius keyframe morph. These blobs must be layered behind all hero content and must be visually distinct from the existing floating pill/capsule SVG shapes already in the hero.

quotes: Make it more animative and designfull and don't repeat:design again

acceptanceCriteria: 6–8 morphing blob shapes are visible at very low opacity behind the hero content on HomePage
Blobs use border-radius keyframe morphing or SVG filter distortion — not the same pill/capsule float used elsewhere
Blobs do not overlap or obscure any text or CTA buttons
Animation cycles are staggered so no two blobs morph in sync
Reduced-motion: blobs render as static shapes without animation

requirements.text: Implement an interactive cursor spotlight effect on the Admin Dashboard pages (AdminDashboardPage, AdminProductsPage, AdminOrdersPage, AdminRefundsPage, AdminSellersPage): a radial gradient glow that follows the mouse cursor across the page background, with a soft 200 px radius emerald glow. This must only activate on desktop (pointer: fine) and must be a new visual layer not shared with any existing glassmorphism, shimmer, or gradient animation already applied to admin pages.

quotes: Make it more animative and designfull and don't repeat:design again

acceptanceCriteria: A radial emerald glow follows the mouse on all five Admin Dashboard pages on desktop
The glow is applied to the page background layer, not on top of content
Effect is disabled on touch/mobile devices (pointer: coarse)
The CSS/JS used is unique and not reused from any other animation in the codebase
prefers-reduced-motion disables the cursor tracking

requirements.text: Add a staggered text word-reveal animation to all major page headings (h1 and prominent section titles) across the Seller Dashboard pages (SellerDashboardPage, SellerProductsPage, SellerOrdersPage, SellerProfilePage): each word in the heading splits and reveals upward from a clip-path mask with a 60 ms stagger per word. This must use a clip-path reveal technique that is distinct from the existing typewriter character-by-character animation already used in the welcome banner.

quotes: Make it more animative and designfull and don't repeat:design again

acceptanceCriteria: All h1 and major section titles on the four Seller Dashboard pages use a word-split clip-path reveal on mount
Each word reveals with a 60 ms delay after the previous word
The animation uses CSS clip-path or overflow-hidden mask — NOT the TypewriterText component
The animation triggers once on page load via IntersectionObserver
Reduced-motion: words appear instantly without reveal animation

requirements.text: Add a unique 'ink-drop' ripple animation to the StatusBadge component (StatusBadge.tsx): whenever a badge's status value changes (e.g., order status updated from pending to confirmed), the badge plays a concentric-ring ink-drop ripple expanding outward from its center before settling on the new color. This is distinct from the existing green-tint table row sweep and pulsing ring badge animations already applied.

quotes: Make it more animative and designfull and don't repeat:design again

acceptanceCriteria: StatusBadge plays a concentric ring ripple animation when its status prop changes
The ripple uses a unique keyframe (outward expanding rings fading to transparent)
Animation duration is 400–600 ms and does not shift layout
The ripple color matches the new status color
prefers-reduced-motion: badge changes color instantly without ripple

requirements.text: Apply a unique visual treatment to the SkeletonProductCard component (SkeletonProductCard.tsx): replace the current shimmer with a scan-line animation — a single bright horizontal line that sweeps top-to-bottom repeatedly over a dark-tinted skeleton, giving a medical-scanner aesthetic. This scan-line must use a different keyframe from the existing shimmer/gradient sweep used on admin glassmorphism cards.

quotes: Make it more animative and designfull and don't repeat:design again

acceptanceCriteria: SkeletonProductCard shows a single bright scan-line sweeping top-to-bottom instead of a shimmer
The scan-line keyframe is unique (translateY-based single line, not a gradient shift)
The skeleton background uses a subtle dark-tinted green tone for the medical-scanner feel
Animation loops continuously while loading
prefers-reduced-motion: static skeleton without scan-line

requirements.text: Add a floating action button (FAB) to the CartPage with a unique spring-bounce entrance animation: the FAB appears in the bottom-right corner only when the cart has items, reveals with a spring overshoot bounce (scale 0 → 1.2 → 1.0), and shows a quick-checkout icon. On click it scrolls to the checkout summary section. This FAB must be visually and animatively distinct from the existing ScrollToTopButton component.

quotes: Make it more animative and designfull and don't repeat:design again

acceptanceCriteria: A FAB appears in the bottom-right of CartPage when cartItems.length > 0
FAB entrance uses a spring overshoot scale animation (0 → 1.2 → 1.0) distinct from the ScrollToTopButton fade-in
FAB displays a checkout/arrow-down icon clearly different from the ScrollToTopButton's up-arrow
Clicking FAB smoothly scrolls to the order summary/checkout section
FAB disappears with a scale-out when cart is empty
prefers-reduced-motion: FAB appears/disappears instantly without spring

constraints: No new animation keyframe or CSS class may duplicate or alias an animation already defined in frontend/src/index.css or frontend/tailwind.config.js
All new animations must include a prefers-reduced-motion media query override
Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Do not introduce new backend changes — all requirements are frontend-only
nonGoals: Re-implementing or replacing any animation that was already delivered in previous features
Adding new pages, routes, or backend functions
Changing any contact information, product data, or business logic
Adding third-party animation libraries not already in the stack
